summary: Merged a broad permuter-driven matching batch across 31 files. The PR body says it was from running permuter on functions and committing outputs that reached 100%; the decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code rising to 65.73% (+0.32%, +12608 bytes), with 45 new matches, 5 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression. Most changes are compiler-shape tactics rather than semantic rewrites: temporary variables, accessor/macro swaps, inline/noinline wrappers, declaration-order changes, stack padding, casts, and small no-op expressions.

key_files.role: Largest diff; expanded memory-card task setup into explicit CardTask writes, introduced lb_80019C38_noinline and lb_8001BB48_inline, used dont_inline pragmas, and matched lb_8001BB48 while the bot reported lb_8001BC18 regressed by 4 bytes.

Improved hsd_803966A0, fn_80397374, and fn_80393C14 by changing global ParticleScreenState access, local placement, and arithmetic shape; bot still listed these as unmatched improvements rather than full matches.
Several Kirby hat SpecialN helper functions reached 100% by using HSD_GObjGetUserData instead of GET_FIGHTER in local setup paths and adding baselib/forward.h.
fn_80122B54 reached 100% through extra gobj/fighter temporaries, reloaded user_data after Fighter_ChangeMotionState/ftPp_set_cbs, and used 0L for the NULL argument.
Camera_8002FEEC reached 100% by splitting the sqrt input expression into a separate float temporary before division.
Used declaration hoisting/order changes in fn_801D7700 and an unused local/comma-expression shape in grKongo_801D8078 for stage matching.
Added grOldYoshi_8020EFCC_inline returning raw user_data, with an explicit comment that normal GET_GROUND did not match there.
Matched it_802B99A0 and it_802BA3BC through local declaration order and assignment-timing tweaks.
Matched boomerang physics helpers by switching to GET_ITEM, separating attribute setup, and adding self-assignment on attrs->xC.
fn_803AC634 reached 100% by splitting the remaining calculation into two statements and removing the old 97.73% TODO.
un_803207C4 reached 100% by wrapping GObj_Create in inline_fn and carrying a second gobj temporary through object/link/proc setup.
HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC reached 100% by using GET_COBJ(gobj) for HSD_CObjSetCurrent; another hunk initialized cur from gobj->hsd_obj.

what_changed: Touched 31 files with 267 added and 132 deleted lines across camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, item, lb, mn, vi, and sysdolphin/baselib code.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code at 65.73%, up 0.32% and +12608 bytes.

Bot reported 45 new matches, including it_802B99A0, fn_8016CA68, fn_80122B54, lb_8001BB48, it_802BBD64, lb_800192A8, un_803207C4, several ftKb_SpecialN hat functions, fn_80165E7C, fn_8024FC48, grOldYoshi_8020EFCC, fn_803AC634, Camera_8002FEEC, grKongo_801D8078, itLinkboomerang_UnkMotion1_Phys, itLinkboomerang_UnkMotion2_Phys, it_802BA3BC, it_802C519C, HSD_SObjLib_803A54EC, lbSnap_8001D4A4, grMuteCity_801F2C10, grFourside_801F3B70, and grGreatBay_801F660C.
Bot reported 5 improvements in still-unmatched items: particle hsd_803966A0, particle fn_80397374, gm_18A5 fn_801981A0, ftCo_Guard .sdata2, and particle fn_80393C14.
Bot reported 1 regression in an unmatched item: lbcardnew lb_8001BC18 went from 96.62% to 95.28% (-4 bytes).
lbcardnew matching was driven by explicit CardTask construction, lb_80019C38_noinline, lb_8001BB48_inline, setup_task replacement in selected paths, and dont_inline pragmas around lb_8001BE30.
Fighter and item changes often toggled between direct user_data, GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, HSD_GObjGetUserData, and HSD_GObjGetHSDObj forms to alter emitted code.
Several functions gained compiler-shape artifacts such as new_var locals, PAD_STACK, self-assignments, comma expressions, explicit casts, and local reordering.
smart_moves: The PR used the decomp.dev match report as a gate: the author explicitly stated they would commit permuter outputs that reached 100%, and the bot confirmed many new matches.
Inline/noinline wrapper functions were used surgically instead of globally changing helper behavior, especially in lbcardnew and stage/menu helpers.
Complex arithmetic was split into staged temporaries where register allocation mattered, e.g. Camera_8002FEEC sqrt input, fn_803AC634 remaining, gm_18A5 x_pos, and particle spr_off math.

Accessor choice was treated as a codegen knob: GET_FIGHTER helped ftCo_Guard, HSD_GObjGetUserData helped Kirby hat functions, GET_ITEM helped Link boomerang, and raw user_data helped some ground functions.
The patch preserved local matching artifacts close to the affected functions, making later cleanup easier to audit against specific matches.
The automated regression was visible in the PR bot output, which is useful for future tradeoff checks around lb_8001BC18.
decomp_lessons: For late-stage matches, macro expansion choice can matter as much as logic; try GET_* macros, raw user_data, and HSD_GObjGetUserData/GET_COBJ variants before restructuring larger code.
Inlining control can be localized with small wrappers named after the target function plus _inline or _noinline, and with targeted #pragma dont_inline blocks.
Expression tree shape affects PowerPC register allocation: splitting one expression into two assignments can close small gaps without changing intended logic.
Declaration order and scope remain important; grKongo, grGreatBay, grVenom, itsamusgrapple, and particle all used local placement/order changes as matching levers.
PAD_STACK, dummy locals, self-assignments, comma expressions, and literal-width casts are legitimate last-mile matching tools, but should be documented as scaffolding rather than semantic insight.
Watch for match-progress tradeoffs: this PR gained many full matches but knowingly left an automated lbcardnew regression in an unmatched item.
Permuter output can create odd-looking but evidence-backed source. Future cleanup should preserve exact matches or re-run decomp.dev before normalizing style.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-based names such as fn_80122B54, fn_80165E7C, fn_8024FC48, hsd_803966A0, and lb_8001BB48.
Local wrapper names used a simple suffix convention: lb_80019C38_noinline, lb_8001BB48_inline, grFlatzone_80216F48_inline, grOldYoshi_8020EFCC_inline, fn_8024FC48_inline, and inline_fn.
Temporary permuter artifacts used non-semantic names like new_var, new_var2, new_var4, fp2, and unused1; these should not be treated as recovered variable names.
The diff reinforces existing subsystem prefixes: ftCo/ftKb/ftPp for fighter code, gr* for stages, it* for items, lb* for library/memory-card helpers, and hsd*/HSD_* for baselib.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split expressions into temporaries: Camera_8002FEEC new_var before sqrtf__Ff, fn_803AC634 remaining assignment split, gm_18A5 x_pos staged assignment, and particle spr_off staged subtraction.
Use accessor/macro alternatives as codegen switches: gobj->user_data, GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, GET_GROUND, HSD_GObjGetUserData, GET_JOBJ, HSD_GObjGetHSDObj, and GET_COBJ.

Control inlining with wrappers and pragmas: lb_80019C38_noinline, lb_8001BB48_inline, lb_8001BE30 #pragma dont_inline, grFlatzone_80216F48_inline, grOldYoshi_8020EFCC_inline, fn_8024FC48_inline, and vi inline_fn.

Insert no-op or dummy code when needed for code shape: attrs->xC = attrs->xC, attr->x18 = attr->x18, cur->pos.z = cur->pos.z, an empty lb_804D63D0 condition, comma expressions like (0, item_pos.z), and dummy locals such as unused1.
Tune stack/register layout with PAD_STACK(8) and by moving variable declarations between inner and outer scopes.
Use explicit literal/cast forms to influence ABI/codegen: (long long) arg1 in gm_16AE, 0L in ftPp_SpecialS, and a wide mask expression in particle hsd_80393328.
Rewrite loop form when it changes emitted code: lbSnap_8001D4A4 changed while loops to for loops and cached ptr->num in new_var.

Reload or alias pointers around calls that may affect register lifetimes, as in ftPp_SpecialS reloading gobj->user_data after state/callback setup and lbcardnew expanding setup_task calls into explicit task pointer writes.
review_feedback: No human review comments or GitHub review records were present in the PR slice.
The only feedback-like record was the decomp.dev bot report: 45 new matches, 5 unmatched improvements, and 1 unmatched regression.
Automated regression to follow up: main/melee/lb/lbcardnew lb_8001BC18 lost 4 bytes of match progress, 96.62% to 95.28%.
